elements.title: Alide Topp
elements.contents: ... that the pianist Alide Topp (pictured) once responded to claims that women lacked the "biceps of a man" for great music by stating she broke her pianos as well as any man?

elements.title: Tlingit
elements.contents: ... that the first writing system for Tlingit, a Native American language, was based on the Cyrillic alphabet?

elements.title: Stardew Valley
elements.contents: ... that some players who obtained Stardew Valley illegally were so impressed they planned to buy it?

elements.title: a 1987 referendum
elements.contents: ... that a 1987 referendum to repeal Mississippi's unenforceable interracial marriage ban was opposed by more than 48 percent of voters?

elements.title: Clear Usui
elements.contents: ... that VTuber Clear Usui is the ambassador of Japan's satellite navigation system?

elements.title: Quatre instants
elements.contents: ... that Kaija Saariaho's Quatre instants have been described as "a quasi-operatic monologue" in "erotic, extreme territory"?

elements.title: Peter Suder
elements.contents: ... that Peter Suder said that the toughest basketball player he had faced was a National Football League player?

elements.title: a war against Ava
elements.contents: ... that King Binnya Ran launched a war against Ava after his sister fled the Ava capital?

elements.title: Courtaud
elements.contents: ... that Courtaud, an infamous man-eating beast described as a "terrible and horrible wolf", was named for its stumpy tail?
